name: Switch Transformer
training_approach: pretraining with sparse MoE layers replacing dense FFN layers
lineage_path: MoE concept (Jacobs 1991) -> Sparsely-Gated MoE (Shazeer 2017) -> Switch Transformer
summary: Switch Transformer simplified sparse mixture-of-experts routing by sending each token to a single expert instead of multiple, dramatically reducing communication costs while scaling to over a trillion parameters. Developed at Google Brain, it demonstrated that MoE could provide 4-7x speedups over dense models at equivalent computational cost, validating sparse expert routing as a practical scaling strategy. It laid the groundwork for the MoE architectures later adopted by GPT-4, Mixtral, and DeepSeek.
